extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial drop (around time 10–15), the series stays roughly flat or slightly rising in the middle section (time 20–40) without strong oscillations.
R2: In the late section (time 45–55), the series dips to a clear local minimum below about 0.2.
R3: After this dip, the recovery in the tail (time 55–70) is modest, ending around mid‑range values (about 0.35–0.5) without a tall peak.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the middle section (time 20–40), the series shows a noticeable bump: it rises to a local maximum near or above 0.5 and then falls back, forming a hill‑like shape.
R2: In the late section (time 45–55), the series again dips to a clear local minimum below about 0.2, similar to class 0.
R3: After this dip, the recovery in the tail (time 55–70) is stronger and more pronounced: the series climbs to and/or maintains higher values (often above 0.5) with a visible peak near the end.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal starts high (above 0.6) and drops relatively quickly to a low valley (around or below 0.25) within the first 10–15 time steps.  
R2: In the mid-section (roughly time 15–45), the signal stays in a moderate band, mostly between 0.35 and 0.6, without any very sharp, tall peak (it is more plateau‑like or gently varying).  
R3: In the final section (after about time 45), the signal remains low‑to‑moderate (typically below 0.55) and does not form a clear, isolated tall peak above 0.65.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal also starts high (above 0.6) and drops to a low valley (around or below 0.25) within the first 10–15 time steps, similar to Class 0.  
R2: In the mid-section (roughly time 25–45), there is a pronounced, isolated peak where the value clearly exceeds 0.7, standing out compared with surrounding points.  
R3: After this tall mid‑section peak, the signal again rises to moderate values (around 0.45–0.6) near the end, but without another peak as tall as the mid‑section one.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the early segment (around index 0–15), the series starts high (above ~0.6) and then quickly drops to a clear local minimum (around or below ~0.25).  
R2: In the middle segment (around index 20–45), the series forms a broad, relatively flat plateau, staying in the mid-range (~0.4–0.6) without sharp spikes.  
R3: In the late segment (around index 50–70), the series rises from a low value (<~0.25) to a mid-level (~0.4–0.5) and then stabilizes or slightly oscillates around that mid-level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the early segment (around index 0–15), the series again starts high and drops low, but the minimum is slightly higher (typically above ~0.2) and reached via a more gradual, oscillatory decline (more small wiggles).  
R2: In the middle segment (around index 20–45), the series shows more pronounced up–down oscillations within the mid-range (~0.4–0.7), including at least one sharper peak (above ~0.7).  
R3: In the late segment (around index 50–70), the series rises strongly from a low (~0.15–0.25) to a higher plateau (often ≥~0.5), ending at or above this higher mid-level rather than settling back near ~0.4.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial drop (around time 5–10), the series stays mostly in a mid-range band (about 0.25–0.55) without a very deep dip close to 0.1.  
R2: The highest peak in the second half of the series is moderate (below roughly 0.75).  
R3: Toward the end (last quarter of time), the values stabilize in a narrow band around the mid-range (roughly 0.35–0.55) without a sharp rebound from a deep minimum.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial drop (around time 5–10), the series contains at least one pronounced deep minimum (near 0.1) in the middle or later part of the series.  
R2: Following such a deep minimum, the series exhibits a strong rebound to a high peak (above roughly 0.7).  
R3: The later part of the series (after the rebound) remains relatively elevated (often above about 0.45) compared with the deep minimum that precedes it.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial high peak, the early drop (around indices 5–15) is relatively shallow, never going clearly below about 0.15.  
R2: The mid‑section (indices ~20–45) is moderately high and relatively flat/plateau‑like, mostly between 0.4 and 0.55 with only gentle wiggles.  
R3: The deepest low occurs late (after index ~45), but its minimum is modest (around 0.15), followed by a recovery that stays below about 0.55.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial high peak, the early drop (around indices 5–15) is deeper than in class 0, reaching close to or clearly below about 0.15.  
R2: The mid‑section (indices ~20–45) is more uneven than in class 0, with a noticeable dip back toward lower values (around or below 0.35) between two higher humps.  
R3: The deepest low occurs late (after index ~45), reaches very low values (close to 0.1), and is followed by a strong rebound to relatively high values (around or above 0.55).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first segment (roughly time 0–10), the signal shows a single sharp peak above 0.7 followed by a fast drop below 0.3.  
R2: Around the middle (roughly time 20–45), the signal stays relatively flat or gently varying in the mid-range, mostly between 0.35 and 0.55, without very sharp spikes.  
R3: After a deep dip below 0.2 around time 50, the last segment (after ≈55) recovers only moderately and stays below about 0.55.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first segment (roughly time 0–10), the signal also peaks high (above 0.7) and drops, but the low part is more oscillatory (multiple small wiggles) rather than a single smooth dip.  
R2: Around the middle (roughly time 20–45), the signal forms a more pronounced “bump,” rising clearly above 0.55 at least once before dropping again.  
R3: After a deep dip below 0.2 around time 50, the last segment (after ≈55) rises more strongly and sustains values close to or above 0.55 for several steps.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial peak and drop (around time 10), the signal rises into a mid‑level plateau (approximately 0.4–0.5) and stays relatively flat for a noticeable interval (around times 20–35) with only small oscillations.
R2: Following this plateau, there is a single broad hump that reaches a moderate‑to‑high value (about 0.6–0.7) and then declines smoothly.
R3: In the late part of the series (after time ~45), the signal remains mostly at low‑to‑mid values (below about 0.5) without forming another tall, narrow spike.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial peak and drop (around time 10), the signal does not settle into a flat plateau; instead it forms a more irregular “bumpy” region with several small peaks and dips between about 0.3 and 0.6.
R2: In the middle of the series (around times 35–45) there is a pronounced, narrow spike that reaches a clearly higher value than the earlier mid‑segment bumps (around 0.75–0.8).
R3: After this central spike, the signal again shows a noticeable rise in the later part (after time ~55) into mid‑range values (around 0.5–0.6), rather than staying low and flat.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The value at the end of the series (last 5–10 time steps) is relatively high, typically above 0.40.  
R2: The lowest valley in the second half of the series (after about one‑third of the length) does not drop below 0.10.  
R3: The maximum peak in the first half of the series is clearly below 0.80.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The value at the end of the series (last 5–10 time steps) is moderate, typically between 0.30 and 0.60.  
R2: There is at least one deep valley in the second half of the series (after about one‑third of the length) that drops close to 0.10 or below 0.15.  
R3: The maximum peak anywhere in the series often reaches around or above 0.75.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The global maximum occurs in the first half of the series (roughly before the midpoint in time).
R2: After that early maximum, the series never reaches a higher value again (later peaks stay below this first-half maximum).
R3: The late section (final quarter in time) shows moderate variability but no sharp, isolated spike higher than the mid‑series level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The global maximum occurs in the second half of the series (roughly after the midpoint in time).
R2: Before this late maximum, there is an earlier peak of medium height (about mid‑series) that is clearly lower than the final peak.
R3: The very end of the series (final quarter in time) recovers to medium values after the late maximum, instead of declining and staying low.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial steep drop (around time 0–10), the series stays mostly between 0.3 and 0.5 for a long middle segment (roughly time 15–45) with only modest oscillations.
R2: In the late part of the series (roughly time 45–60), there is a pronounced dip close to 0.1 followed by a recovery but without exceeding about 0.55.
R3: Across the whole series, there is no very sharp, tall peak in the late segment: values after time 40 never rise above roughly 0.7.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial steep drop (around time 0–10), the series again has a middle segment (roughly time 15–40) oscillating around 0.4–0.6, similar to class 0.
R2: In the late part of the series (roughly time 40–55), there is a very sharp, tall spike: the value quickly rises from about 0.3–0.5 to near or above 0.75 and then falls back down.
R3: After this tall late spike, the values stay in a moderate band around 0.4–0.6 without another deep dip to 0.1.
